title: cephalotaxus fortunei subsp. alpina
intro: Cephalotaxus fortunei subsp. alpina, also known as the Chinese plum yew, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous shrub or small tree native to China and the Himalayas. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its alkaloids, particularly cephalotaxine, which have shown potential in treating certain types of cancer and other diseases. Cephalotaxus fortunei subsp. alpina is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as extracts and infusions, are used in traditional medicine, although their use should be cautious due to potential toxicity and side effects.
